_meta.principle: DHH-native slang explained for the curious. Hindi / Punjabi / Marathi / Mumbaiya / Urdu mixed.

terms.track: Emiway's stage name and his label both lean into the word.

terms.meaning: real, authentic. The opposite of fake.
terms.track: Asli Action Chaalu (Gully Gang) is a whole anthem on the word.

terms.meaning: narrow lane, the back streets. Spatial AND social: where the real music came from before the radio noticed.
terms.track: Mere Gully Mein (Divine + Naezy) named the wave.

terms.meaning: the famous diamond. In rap, slang for the brightest thing in the room or your most prized possession.
terms.track: Divine's Kohinoor album positioned him as the centerpiece of the wave.

terms.meaning: we'll smash it, we'll set it off. Future-tense flex.
banished, exiled. Specifically the legal/colonial term for being run out of town.
Indian Penal Code Section 295A — outraging religious feelings. Sidhu used the code as a track title to flip the law on its head.
1997 Doordarshan superhero. Used in rap as the patron saint of growing up Indian in the 90s.
brother. Mumbai street form of respect.
tigress. Used for fearless women in rap.
terms.track: Featured across multiple tracks in the new wave.

terms.meaning: virtue and sin. The moral ledger.
swagger, command, presence. The vibe you walk in with.
fog. Often used for moral haze.
terms.track: Yashraj · Dhundhala plays with the same register.

terms.meaning: greatest of all time. The argument every Top 5 list is implicitly making.
